pattern: Swap pairs link indices into connected components within which values are freely rearrangeable, so each component independently assigns its largest values to the plus positions and smallest to the minus positions.
hints: Repeated swaps let any two indices in the same swap-connected group exchange values, so think in terms of connected components of indices.
Inside one component you may place its collected values into its positions in any order; each position carries a fixed plus or minus sign based on its parity.

Union the indices joined by swaps, then per component count how many positions are even (plus); sort the component's values descending and give the top ones to the plus positions and the rest to the minus positions.

edge_inputs.description: two swaps connect all three indices
single swap between two odd-parity-mixed indices
large values with no swaps gives negative result
swap connects two even positions, no sign change benefit
chain of swaps forms one big component
two separate components each optimized independently

stress.description: random values with random swap edges so components of varied parity mixes form
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    m = rng.randint(0, n)
    seen = set()
    swaps = []
    for _ in range(m):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a == b:
            continue
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        if (a, b) in seen:
            continue
        seen.add((a, b))
        swaps.append([a, b])
    return [nums, swaps]
